steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The figure shows a right triangle with one angle of $90^{\circ}$ and one angle of $45^{\circ}$. The three interior angles of any triangle sum to $180^{\circ}$:
$$90 + 45 + x = 180$$
$$135 + x = 180$$
$$x = 45$$

steps.title: Confirm with the isosceles right triangle property
steps.content:
Because two angles are equal ($45^{\circ} = 45^{\circ}$), the triangle is isosceles. The two legs opposite the equal angles are congruent, which is consistent with a 45-45-90 right triangle.

Therefore $x = $ 45.
